title: Script Writer for Meta Ads
description:
About Us

We're a fast-growing Scandinavian e-commerce brand scaling aggressively with Meta ads. We have winning creatives already running — now we need someone who can help us build the next batch.

We don't need someone who writes pretty copy. We need someone who understands why people buy and can translate that into scripts that stop the scroll and convert.



What You'll Be Doing



Writing scripts for short-form video ads (30–60 sec) based on our creative briefs

Crafting hooks, body copy, and CTAs tailored to specific customer avatars

Working across multiple angles per batch — problem aware, solution aware, emotional

Scripts are written in English — our team handles translation and voiceover





You Are Probably a Fit If...



You've written scripts specifically for Meta ads that have generated sales — not just views

You understand concepts like hook rate, problem/solution aware, and direct-response copy

You think about the customer first, not the product

You've read books like $100M Offers, Breakthrough Advertising, or similar

You can write conversationally — short sentences, no fluff, reads like a human wrote it





You Are NOT a Fit If...



Your portfolio is only organic social content or brand awareness copy

You write formal, polished English that sounds like a press release

You've never seen or cared about ad metrics like hook rate, CTR, or ROAS





How to Apply

No generic applications. We will ignore them.

Send us:



2–3 Meta ad scripts you've written that generated real results — what was the product, what was the angle, what happened?

Write a hook (one sentence, text-on-screen format) for this product: a wrist blood pressure monitor sold to adults aged 35–55 who are worried about an aging parent

Answer this: What's the difference between a problem aware and solution aware customer — and how does that change how you write the ad?



Question 3 is the filter. If you can't answer it, this role isn't for you.

title: GoHighLevel / Wordpress Specialist — Funnels, Automations & CRM
description:
I'm looking for an expert GoHighLevel specialist for a full infrastructure build. This is for a course-based equity jurisprudence education brand with active students and live revenue. The system needs to be built right the first time — no template drops, no guesswork.



Here's what I need built:



CRM & PIPELINES

- Full contact migration (~400 alumni) into GHL

- Pipeline architecture: Prospect ? Warm Lead ? Enrolled ? Active Student ? Alumni ? Referral Partner

- Tag and segment by course type, payment plan, covenant status, and engagement

- Private coaching pipeline tracking documents, deadlines, and session history

- Referral partner tracking with credit balance fields



/SMS AUTOMATIONS

- Pre-enrollment nurture sequence 
 + SMS)

- Post-enrollment onboarding sequence

- Zoom class reminders: 24hr / 6hr / 1hr / live-now 
 + SMS)

- Re-engagement campaigns for cold leads

- Abandoned cart / incomplete checkout recovery

- Post-course upsell sequence

- Covenant follow-up sequence (days 2, 5, 10 if unsigned)



SALES FUNNELS & LANDING PAGES

- Multi-step quiz funnel with lead scoring and conditional routing (high-fit ? calendar, low-fit ? nurture)

- Course enrollment funnel: opt-in ? VSL/webinar ? checkout ? confirmation ? upsell/downsell

- Lead magnet opt-in pages

- Webinar reminder sequences



PAYMENT INTEGRATION

- Stripe integration (existing)

- Easy Pay Direct integration (primary card processor — being onboarded)

- 3-payment plan access gating (release content per payment milestone)

- Payment upgrade path (3PP ? full one-time ? immediate full access trigger)



WEBSITE (Phase 4)

- Full WordPress site build

- SEO-optimized (Yoast)

- Hosted on Iceland-based VPS (OrangeWebsite)

- GHL forms embedded throughout

- VDO Cipher iframe integration for video content



Before we move forward, I need to know:



1. How many GHL builds have you completed? Please share 2–3 live links — not Google Drive screenshots, actual URLs I can click and evaluate.

2. Have you built course enrollment funnels with payment-gated content access? Describe specifically how you handled it.

3. Have you built multi-step quiz funnels with conditional routing in GHL? Walk me through how you approach the logic.

4. Are you comfortable with Stripe AND Easy Pay Direct integration inside GHL?

5. Have you worked with WordPress + Elementor alongside a GHL build? (Both are required for this project.)

6. What is your turnaround time for a full funnel + automation build at this scope?

7. Do you offer revisions and post-launch support?

8. What is your rate? Build fee + monthly retainer if applicable.



Budget: $1,750 for the initial build. Ongoing retainer available at $750/month for the right person.



This is a real business with active students. I need someone with proven experience, clean automation logic, and the ability to manage a complex multi-phase build without hand-holding.



If this is in your wheelhouse, send your answers, live portfolio links, and include the word "covenant" somewhere in your response so I know you read this carefully.



Thanks.

title: CRO Account Manager – E-commerce
description:
We are seeking a CRO Account Manager to own client relationships and lead delivery across CRO and web projects for ambitious eCommerce and Shopify brands. 



This is not a passive coordination role. You will drive outcomes, set direction, create clarity, remove blockers, and keep delivery moving at pace.



Key Responsibilities



1) Client Leadership

-Be the primary point of contact for key accounts.

-Lead calls confidently, with structure and clear outcomes.

-Translate business goals into CRO strategy, roadmaps, and execution plans.

-Proactively manage expectations, risks, and priorities.

-Build long-term trust through reliability, honesty, and strong communication.





2) Delivery & Project Execution

-Run delivery in ClickUp: scoping, prioritisation, timelines, and accountability.

-Write clear briefs that designers and developers can execute without confusion.

-QA design and development output to ensure accuracy, polish, and alignment with CRO goals.

-Keep work organised, time-bound, and commercially aligned—no drifting scope.





3) CRO Strategy, Audits & Roadmapping

-Conduct mini and full CRO audits (qualitative and quantitative) and turn insights into clear priorities.

-Build CRO roadmaps outlining what we’re doing, why, and how success is measured.

-Plan experiments and site improvements that drive CVR, AOV, LTV, and funnel performance.

-Spot and communicate upsell opportunities that genuinely align with client growth.



4) Pod Leadership (Design + Dev)

-Lead pods of designers and developers.

-Set expectations, push for standards, and drive accountability.

-Keep teams aligned, motivated, and moving quickly.

-Improve systems and SOPs as we scale.



This role is ideal for someone charismatic, sharp, and intensely organised. You thrive on responsibility, take ownership, and finish what you start. You can manage client relationships with confidence and run delivery with discipline.



Requirements

-3+ years experience in eCommerce, websites, or Shopify.

-2+ years experience in conversion rate optimization (CRO).

-2+ years experience as an account manager (agency or similar).

-Experience leading delivery with designers and developers.

-Strong understanding of eCommerce metrics: CVR, AOV, LTV, funnel performance, and experimentation logic.

-Comfortable owning client relationships.

-Strong briefing and QA skills.

title: Graphic Designer (Document & Brand Design)
description:
-ABOUT US-



We're Pure Proposals, a PandaDoc consulting agency that helps businesses build professional proposal and quoting systems. We work with clients across Australia, the US, Canada, New Zealand, and the UK. Our clients range from entertainment venue groups to construction companies to SaaS businesses. We need a designer who can make their documents look incredible.



What You'll Be Designing



Document and proposal designs - this is the core of the role. We build proposal templates, quotes, contracts, and booking confirmations inside a platform called PandaDoc for our clients. You don't need to know PandaDoc. Your job is to design the layouts in Figma or your preferred design tool, and our team implements them into the platform. You'll take a client's brand and create polished, professional document designs that we then bring to life. What matters is your eye for layout, typography, and clean document design.



Social media graphics - LinkedIn infographics, carousels, and post graphics. We need someone who can take a topic or set of data points and turn them into visually compelling, shareable graphics. Not templated filler. Think the kind of infographic or carousel that makes someone stop scrolling because it's genuinely useful and looks great. We'll provide the content and direction, you make it visual.



Case studies and one-pagers - single or multi-page PDFs that showcase client results. Clean, modern layout with strong visual hierarchy.



Ebooks and guides - longer-form designed documents (10-20 pages) for lead magnets and client resources.

Website design assets - banners, graphics, and visual elements for our Framer website.



Brand collateral - helping consolidate and maintain our brand guidelines, creating consistent assets across all touchpoints.



What We're Looking For



Must-haves:



Strong portfolio showing document layout and design (proposals, pitch decks, reports, case studies, or similar). This is the most important thing. If your portfolio is all logos and social media posts, this isn't the right fit.



Clean, modern design aesthetic. Think rounded edges, bold but not overwhelming colours, strong typography, plenty of white space. We like designs that feel premium without being overly corporate.



Proficiency in Figma and/or Canva. Adobe suite is a bonus but not required.



Responsive communication. We move fast and need someone who replies within a few hours during working hours.

Comfortable working asynchronously with clear briefs via Slack.



Nice-to-haves:



Experience with infographic design or data visualization

Experience with presentation/pitch deck design

Basic web design skills (Framer, Webflow, or similar)



How to Apply

Send us:



1. A link to your portfolio. This is the most important part of your application. We're evaluating your design style and quality before anything else. Show us document layouts, proposal designs, pitch decks, case studies, brand collateral. The more relevant to what we described above, the better.



2. A short message (3-4 sentences max) telling us: your timezone, your hourly rate, and your typical availability per week.



3. One example from your portfolio that you think best represents the style of work we're describing.



We will not respond to applications without a portfolio link.



Put PROPOSALS at the start of your application. If you don't it will be automatically rejected.

title: Revit Families BIM Modeler (Remote)
description:
Company: RD Studio (rdstudio.co)

Employment Type: Full-Time Freelance

Location: Remote 



RD Studio is an international BIM content company known for high-quality Revit families used by thousands of architects worldwide. We are expanding our team and looking for a Revit Family Modeler who specializes in complex, highly parametric content.



(Note: We are specifically looking for advanced modelers. If you are a beginner or only build basic geometry without nested parameters, this role is not for you).



-----------------------------

? What You’ll Do



Build complex parametric content: Create professional, lightweight Revit families from scratch using nested families, complex formulas, and advanced constraints.

Maintain strict quality: Follow our internal BIM standards for geometry, parameters, materials, and visibility.

Draft diverse assets: Work on furniture, plumbing fixtures, lighting, electrical, structural, and more.

Collaborate efficiently: Manage your workflow using Asana, Google Sheets, and shared QA checklists.



-----------------------------

What We’re Looking For



Advanced Parametric Experience: Proven ability to build highly flexible families (portfolio and .RFA samples are required).

Technical Mastery: Deep understanding of shared parameters, reference planes, type catalogs, and mathematical/logical formulas.

Professional Organization: Ability to structure and name parameters cleanly and logically.

Work Ethic: Strong attention to detail, proactive problem-solving, and comfortable with English documentation.

Bonus: Experience modeling MEP or Structural content.



-----------------------------

Why Join Us?



100% Remote: Work from home with flexible hours.

Career Progression: Long-term collaboration with the potential to grow into a Senior Modeler or QA Leadership role.



-----------------------------

Location & Budget

-----------------------------

How to Apply

1. Send an
 to 
 with the subject: Revit Family Modeler — [Your Name]

This is not a short-term project — we want someone we can trust and build with over time. There is real room to grow in this role.

What you'll be doing



SHOPIFY STORE MANAGEMENT

Translate and localise product titles, descriptions, and pages for different markets

Set up and configure stores for new markets (currencies, regions, shipping, etc.)

CUSTOMER SUPPORT

Manage customer
s and support tickets across multiple language markets

Maintain fast response times and a high level of customer satisfaction



DAILY REPORTING & BOOKKEEPING

Keep records accurate and organised across all stores and markets

Flag any discrepancies or unusual trends for review

What we're looking for



Strong English required — written and spoken. You will be handling customer
s, translating content, and communicating with our team daily. Poor English will result in immediate disqualification.

Proven experience with Shopify — theme editing, product listings, store settings

Experience editing videos in CapCut (text swaps, subtitle edits)

Previous customer support experience, ideally in ecommerce

Comfortable working across multiple languages (or working with translation tools accurately)

What we offer

How to apply

title: Graphic Designer
description:
About the Role



We’re a boutique branding studio based in the Netherlands, working at the intersection of identity, systems, and design execution. We’re looking for a Brand & UI Systems Designer — someone who can translate brand identity into structured, scalable visual assets across decks, templates, interfaces, and documentation.



This is a part-time remote role (3 fixed days/week) with the potential to grow into a full-time position. You’ll work on both client-facing and internal design systems — creating clarity, consistency, and structure in everything you build.



If you enjoy making brands work — not just look good — this is for you.



---



What You’ll Do



- Apply and extend brand systems across digital and print applications

- Design structured layouts, branded decks, templates, and documentation visuals

- Build and maintain brand kits, internal design tools, and asset libraries

- Create light UI components and visuals that support interface design (e.g., Webflow mockups, content visuals)

- Work independently but collaboratively — with support from our design and strategy team

- Use Figma, Illustrator, and InDesign as your core tools

- Keep all files organized, scalable, and developer-ready where needed



---



What This Role is Not



This is not a generalist or social media content role.



It’s also not for junior designers focused purely on visual trends or aesthetics.



We’re looking for someone who:



- Thinks in systems, not just single-use visuals

- Builds brand architecture, not just one-off designs

- Can adapt identity into interface and layout logic



---



What We’re Looking For



- 5+ years of experience in branding, identity design, or UI-aligned visual systems

- Strong portfolio showing brand system implementation across multiple touchpoints

- Experience creating and managing assets like design kits, templates, and documentation

- Confident with tools like Figma, Illustrator, and InDesign

- Good habits around file structure, versioning, and organized delivery

- Excellent English (C1/C2 level) — both written and spoken

- Available to work 3 fixed days/week (6–8 hours/day)

- Comfortable working independently and owning deliverables



---



Nice to Have



- Experience with Shopify (designing assets, layout support)

- Familiarity with Webflow or other no-code platforms

- Proficiency with Premiere Pro and After Effects

- Interest in building internal systems and improving design operations



---



Compensation & Benefits



- Monthly range: 45,000 – 65,000PHP, depending on experience and portfolio 

- Final offer based on your portfolio, profile, and a short design use case test

- Path to grow into a full-time position if the collaboration is strong

- 13th month bonus for long-term contributors (including part-time)

- Be part of a team that values clarity, autonomy, and systems thinking



---



How to Apply



Send us:



- Your portfolio — focused on brand systems, templates, or UI-aligned identity work

- A short motivation explaining why this role fits your skill set and mindset



If there’s a potential fit, we’ll invite you to complete a short design use case as part of the evaluation.

title: Sales Account Executive
description:
How to Apply

* Send your most updated resume to 



About us:



Gushwork.ai builds AI Agents that help small and medium businesses automate their entire inbound marketing stack — from SEO and website content to reviews, blogs, and lead funnels. Our current focus is the manufacturing and industrial distributors industry.



In plain English: we’re replacing bloated marketing agencies with intelligent, always-on software.



Backed by Lightspeed, SIG, Beenext, and B Capital, Gushwork.ai is one of the fastest-growing AI companies globally.



In just a year, we’ve grown to $1.5M ARR and are scaling fast toward $15M ARR in the next 12 months — helping thousands of small businesses grow smarter, not harder.



The Role:



We’re looking for a closer who thrives in fast-paced, high-velocity sales environments. You’ll own the full sales cycle from discovery to close for qualified meetings booked by our 10-person BDR team. This is a quota-carrying role where you’ll close 4-8 deals monthly, with deal cycles ranging from 1-3 calls.



Working Hours: US Shift (9:00 AM - 6:00 PM EST)

Work Mode: Remote



What You’ll Do:



- Run discovery calls with qualified prospects (OEMs, distributors, manufacturers) to uncover pain points around lead generation and digital presence

- Deliver compelling demos that connect our AI SEO solution to specific business outcomes and revenue impact

- Navigate buying committees within SMB organizations, typically engaging with Marketing Directors, Sales Leaders, and C-suite

- Close deals on 12-month contracts with ACV of $5k-$25k, meeting or exceeding monthly quota

- Manage pipeline with discipline—forecast accurately, maintain detailed CRM hygiene, and move deals forward with urgency

- Partner with Growth Managers post-sale to ensure smooth handoffs and identify expansion opportunities

- Iterate on sales methodology by sharing wins/losses and collaborating on improving conversion rates



What Success Looks Like:



- Month 1-2: Ramp on product, market, and sales process; close your first 2-3 deals

- Month 3+: Consistently hit/exceed quota of 4-8 closed deals monthly

- Ongoing: Maintain 30%+ demo-to-close conversion rate and sub-30 day sales cycles



You’re a Great Fit If You Have:



- 2-4 years of closing experience in B2B SaaS, MarTech, or digital services

- Proven track record hitting/exceeding quota in a high-velocity sales environment

- SMB sales chops—you know how to build urgency, handle objections, and close without lengthy procurement cycles

- Consultative selling skills—you diagnose before prescribing and can articulate ROI clearly

- Comfort with ambiguity—you’re joining an early-stage startup where adaptability matters

- CRM proficiency (Zoho, HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.) and strong process discipline

- Excellent English communication skills for engaging with global prospects

- Want to hustle like crazy to hit the numbers!



Bonus Points For:



- Experience selling to manufacturers, distributors, or industrial/B2B companies

- Background in SEO, content marketing, or lead generation solutions

- Track record in 1-3 call close environments

- Familiarity with AI-powered products

- Experience selling to US/international markets



What We Offer:



- Accelerators for over-quota performance—top performers earn significantly more

- Qualified pipeline delivered daily by our BDR team—focus on closing, not prospecting

- Fast feedback loops—short sales cycles mean you learn and earn quickly

- Career growth—as we scale, top performers move into senior AE or team lead roles

- Equity in a VC-backed rocketship

- Collaborative, high-performance culture where wins are celebrated and losses are learning 
nts

- Remote work



Our Sales Process



- BDR books qualified meeting ? hands off to you

- Call 1: Discovery (30-45 min) - understand business, pain points, buying process

- Call 2: Demo + Proposal (45-60 min) - tailored walkthrough, ROI discussion, pricing

- Call 3 (if needed): Negotiation/Close - handle final objections, terms, signatures

- Handoff to Growth Manager for onboarding



How to Apply

* Send your most updated resume to

title: Creative Strategist & Media Buyer
description:
We're building an AI-powered SEO automation platform launching next week. You'll get direct 1-on-1 access to the founder and work daily with our editor.



I'm looking for someone who can own both the creative and paid acquisition side of our growth. That means developing ad concepts, writing copy that converts, building and managing campaigns across Meta and Google, analyzing performance data, and iterating fast. You'll shape the brand voice, create assets, and make sure every dollar we spend on ads is working.

You should have strong creative instincts and know how to turn those into campaigns that actually perform. Experience running paid media for SaaS or tech products is a plus. You need to be comfortable using AI tools as part of your workflow.

This is an early-stage role with real ownership. If you thrive with autonomy and want to build a growth engine from the ground up, this is for you.



How to apply



Start your application with the letter: X

Then answer these two questions:



1. Walk me through one ad or campaign you ran that delivered strong results. What was the strategy, what creative decisions did you make, and what specifically made it work? Don't just send a screenshot; explain your thinking.

How are you using AI in your creative or media buying process today? Be specific about tools, workflows, and where it gives you an edge.



2. Keep your answers clear and specific. Generic responses will be ignored.



P.S. This starts as a contract position on an hourly basis, but strong performers move to full-time. You'll get a base rate plus performance incentives, so if you deliver results, you'll be paid well for it.

title: E-commerce & Shopify Skincare Specialist (with Strong AI / ChatGPT Skills) – Remote
description:
We’re a high-end online skincare and supplements retailer focused on professional, results-driven products. Every detail — from the product catalog to customer communication — is designed to feel premium, expert and trustworthy.



We’re looking for a long-term collaborator who combines Shopify mastery with advanced AI / ChatGPT prompting skills and a precise sense of structure, design and tone.



Role overview

1. Shopify product catalog & structure



Organise and categorise all products accurately (skin type, concern, product type, brand, etc.).



Improve titles, tags, filters and collections for consistency and clarity.



Set up metafields and attributes to support filters, quizzes and product recommendations.



Ensure all pages are clean, mobile-friendly and aligned with a high-end brand aesthetic.



2. Product content & optimisation (with AI support)

Use AI tools (ChatGPT or similar) for first drafts, then refine and perfect manually.



Highlight key benefits, active ingredients and usage instructions.



Add cross-sells and upsells to build routines or complete sets.



Apply basic SEO (headers, meta data, image tags, internal links).



3. AI / ChatGPT workflows & prompting

Product descriptions



Routine and regimen recommendations



 campaigns and automations



Educational or blog-style content

(Not required, but a strong plus)

Use AI to generate logic and text for questions and results.



Work with existing Shopify tools or third-party quiz/chat apps.



5. Merchandising & bestsellers



Highlight key products and bestsellers across the site.



Create product bundles and complete routines.



Continuously improve visibility and UX using store data and analytics.



6.
 marketing



Create high-quality, educational
 campaigns and automations.



Use AI to ideate and draft, then manually refine tone and flow.



Build automated flows (welcome, abandoned cart, replenishment, educational).



Segment lists by customer interests, behaviours and purchase history.



Maintain a refined, trustworthy brand voice throughout.



Key responsibilities



Manage and optimise the full Shopify catalog and structure.



Use AI / ChatGPT daily for copy creation, workflow building and optimisation.



Keep the tone premium, knowledgeable and consistent.



Ensure bestsellers, bundles and routines are clearly presented.



(Optionally) assist in creating a skincare quiz or consultation flow.



Requirements



Proven Shopify experience (send links to stores you’ve worked on).



Strong background in e-commerce, ideally in beauty, wellness or supplements.



Excellent organisational skills and attention to product data detail.



Advanced AI / ChatGPT prompting skills, including:



Clear role/system prompt structuring



Iterative refining for brand voice and style



Building templates for consistent results



Fluent written English and strong copywriting skills.



Understanding of on-page SEO and mobile-friendly design.



Experience with at least one
 platform (e.g. Klaviyo, Mailchimp).



Independent, proactive, self-organising mindset.



Nice to have



Experience with quizzes, chatbots or guided recommendation tools.



Knowledge of skincare ingredients and concerns.



Basic graphic or layout editing (Figma / Canva).



Familiarity with conversion optimisation or analytics.



Additional language skills (especially EU languages).



What’s offered



Long-term remote collaboration.



Work with premium skincare and wellness products.



Freedom to propose improvements and automation ideas.



Direct communication, quick decisions, and creative autonomy.



How to apply



Send:



A short introduction about yourself and your background.



Links to Shopify stores you’ve worked on (with a short description of your role).



Examples of product pages or
 campaigns you created.

title: Wordpress & Shopify & Research
description:
We are looking for a WordPress & Shopify & Research Assistant who will work as a direct extension of the owner. You will support all daily operations related to websites, online stores, SEO, research, creatives and basic order follow-up. The ideal candidate is highly structured, extremely punctual, perfectionistic, and follows instructions exactly as given.



Main Responsibilities

1.
 Ads Library research



Search and analyse winning ads and creatives based on very specific criteria.



Document findings clearly in Google Sheets / Excel.



Follow step-by-step instructions without skipping or changing anything.



2. WordPress design & implementation



Build and edit pages in WordPress (Elementor or similar page builders).



Implement pixel-perfect layouts from examples or written instructions.



Work confidently with HTML & basic CSS to adjust sections, buttons, layouts, and responsive design.



3. Shopify store management & operations



Create and update products, collections, menus and basic theme settings.



Maintain clean, consistent product pages (titles, descriptions, images, variants, pricing, tags).



Implement upsells, offer blocks and simple Liquid/HTML sections when needed.



Regularly check the store for broken links, layout issues, missing images or errors and fix them.



Assist with order & customer follow-up:



Look up orders, tracking and payment status when there is a question or problem.



Help prepare and follow SOPs for returns, refunds, resends and problem cases, and escalate to the owner when needed.



Keep a clear log of all actions taken on each case.



Act as a full backup for daily Shopify tasks so the owner can step away and the business still runs smoothly.



4. SEO-specific improvements (WordPress & Shopify)



Implement SEO improvements based on analyses and checklists provided by the owner.



Optimise slugs, meta titles, meta descriptions and on-page structure according to a fixed SEO system.



Improve internal linking, headings, image alt text and basic technical SEO elements.



Monitor key pages after changes and keep detailed notes of what was changed and why.



5. Creatives & Canva



Use Canva (and similar tools) to create ad creatives, social media posts, thumbnails and simple web graphics.



Follow the owner’s exact visual guidelines and instructions (fonts, colours, layout, text length, language, brand style).



Adapt existing templates and build new ones when needed.



Export files in the correct sizes, formats and naming structure for ads and websites.



6. General support, AI & research



Competitor research for products, offers, funnels and creatives.



Use AI tools for first drafts of product descriptions, ad angles, blogs and SEO texts, strictly following the owner’s prompts.



Edit and polish AI output so it matches brand tone, structure and SEO rules.



Daily reporting of what has been done, with links and screenshots when useful.



Gradually learn to handle everything the owner can do on WordPress, Shopify, SEO, creatives and research tasks.



Working Style & Expectations



You work as a supporter and extension of the owner, not as a “do it your way” freelancer.



You must be available and reachable during all working hours (chat / voice when needed).



You will work with AnyDesk permanently open during work time, so the owner can see your screen at any 
nt and give live feedback.



You are extremely punctual: you start on time, respect deadlines, and communicate immediately if something blocks you.



You are perfectionistic in a good way: small details matter (spacing, fonts, spelling, slugs, URLs, image names, internal links, etc.).



You follow instructions to the letter: no improvisation unless explicitly requested.



If something is unclear, you ask once, then document the solution for future use (SOP mindset).



Schedule & Availability



Full-time: 8 hours per day, Monday to Friday



Fixed working hours: 11:00 – 19:00 GMT



This corresponds to 12:00 – 20:00 Belgium time.



You must have a stable internet connection and a working environment that allows constant availability during these hours.



Requirements



Strong experience with WordPress design (preferably Elementor or similar builders).



Good knowledge of HTML (and basic CSS) for editing sections, layouts and responsive issues.



Solid experience with Shopify (products, collections, basic theme edits, simple Liquid/HTML, store hygiene).



Experience or strong interest in
 Ads Library research and e-commerce.



Basic to intermediate SEO knowledge, and willingness to strictly follow a provided SEO framework.



Experience using Canva and other tools to create clean, conversion-focused creatives from strict instructions.



Comfortable using AI tools for writing and research and then refining the output.



Good written and spoken English.



Reliable, organised, detail-obsessed, and comfortable being supervised live through AnyDesk.



If you love structure, can follow detailed instructions without changing them, and want to grow long-term with an ambitious e-commerce & web projects owner, this role is for you.

title: Real Estate Video Editor (Part-Time) Final Cut Pro recommended
description:
Charlotte Real Estate Media is looking for part-time video editors to join our growing video team. This is not full-time employment. Editors are assigned projects as jobs come in.



Schedule & Availability

 • Workdays: Monday through Saturday

 • We do not work on Sundays

 • Projects are typically assigned by 10:00 PM EST

 • Videos must be submitted by 7:00 AM EST

 • Editors should be available to work evenings based on this schedule

 • Weekly workload will vary based on volume



Work Structure

 • Editors are assigned projects by a Video Team Leader

 • Projects are assigned as they come in

 • Some weeks may be busier than others

 • If volume slows, fewer projects may be assigned

 • This role is best for someone comfortable with flexible, project-based work



Pay

 • Paid per video ($20 for basic video, $35 for Premium video)

 • Rates discussed during the interview process



Software & Equipment

 • Final Cut Pro is recommended

 • A MacBook is strongly preferred

 • If you do not currently have a MacBook or Final Cut Pro, one may be provided

 • All music, fonts, sound effects, and creative assets are provided

 • Editors are not required to purchase any assets



Requirements

 • Experience editing video (Final Cut Pro or similar NLE)

 • High-speed, reliable internet connection

 • Strong attention to detail (especially spelling and on-screen text)

 • Good written English

 • Ability to follow systems and instructions

 • Team-oriented attitude and good communication



Experience

 • Real estate video editing experience is strongly preferred

 • Applicants must submit at least one example of a real estate video they have edited previously



How to Apply (Important)

When you apply:

 • Include “Mac” or “PC” in the subject line

 • In your message, clearly list your weekly availability (days and hours)

title: Short-Form Video Editor (Real Estate Branding Content)
description:
We’re hiring an additional short-form video editor to work alongside our current editor on real estate branding videos for Realtors.



This is a fast-paced, repeatable editing role. If you’re skilled, dependable, and comfortable working with clear systems and deadlines, this is a great opportunity.





What You’ll Be Editing

 • Short-form Realtor branding videos

 • 30–90 seconds per video

 • Agent-on-camera videos, testimonials, brand stories, and social clips

 • You will be given example links showing the exact style of videos you’ll be creating





Pay

 • $30 USD per completed video

 • Paid per video (contractor role)

 • Volume varies week to week





Turnaround Expectations

 • All videos must be delivered within 24 hours of receiving footage

 • Footage is typically uploaded by 10:00 PM EST

 • Final edits must be uploaded by 9:00 AM EST the following day

 • Most videos should take 2 hours or less once you understand the workflow

 • Timing and reliability are extremely important





Responsibilities

 • Download raw footage

 • Edit clean, engaging short-form videos

 • Use provided music, fonts, and brand guidelines

 • Upload final videos to Vimeo

 • Upload videos into our video delivery portal

Requirements

 • Strong, fast, and reliable internet connection (required)

 • Ability to download and upload large video files quickly

 • Your own computer and editing software

 • Available up to 6 days per week if needed

 • Comfortable working independently and hitting deadlines

 • Teachable, humble, and detail-oriented





VERY IMPORTANT – Test Project (Required)



To be considered for this position, you must complete a short unpaid test project.



This is not free work.

This is the next step in the interview process so we can evaluate skill, speed, and attention to detail. We do a high volume of work and cannot train from scratch.

 • If selected, you will be sent a test project

title: Customer Support (Written) | No Experience Needed | FUN| Dance Academy
description:
***READ FULL JOB POST BEFORE APPLYING. *** Please don't waste your time or ours. Thanks :)



Full-time, long-term opportunity.



Four days per week, or five days per week scheduling options.



Looking for:

- Outstanding English

- Empathy



Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL.



ABOUT US

We are the largest salsa & bachata dance academy in Los Angeles and are growing rapidly.



The culture here is FUN, fast, and friendly. Our goal is to make every student feel welcome and completely taken care of.



Dancing changes lives - we are on a mission to deliver the best salsa/bachata educational experience on Earth.



The company is MaS Dance Academy.





ABOUT YOUR TEAM

Working with a remote team that helps with all facets of the business, you'll form a close working relationship and bond with your team mates. We even host team retreats in the Philippines!



----------------------------

SCHEDULE

All times written in PST (Los Angeles time)



TRAINING (2-4 weeks):

Sunday - Thursday 9am - 6pm (with 1 hour break)



AFTER TRAINING



Both options are full-time 40 hours per week.



OPTION 1 - FIVE DAY SCHEDULE:

Thursday - Monday 8am - 5pm (with 1 hour break)



OPTION 2 - FOUR DAY SCHEDULE:

Saturday, Tuesday, Wednesday 10am - 9pm (with 1 hour break)

Friday 12pm (noon) - 11pm (with 1 hour break)

----------------------------



YOUR ROLE

• Your primary role is written student support (text,
 etc.)

• When there are no support tasks to complete, you'll contribute in a wide variety of other ways, depending on your skills and interests. Some examples may include writing blogs, updating websites, doing research, collecting and analyzing numerical data and using it to create reports, etc.





WRITTEN VS PHONE SUPPORT

• 95% of our student support is written. However, occasionally you will need to be on the phone with a student. You must be comfortable on the phone.





TYPES OF SUPPORT YOU'LL HANDLE

• One on one texting and
ing

• Mass texting and
ing

• Occasional phone calls (rare)

• Updating database after every interaction and attempted interaction

• Daily reports

• Etc.





COMPANY CULTURE

Working in a small business like ours is very different from a large corporation. Some of the differences are:



• Personal tone: Our communications with students are FRIENDLY, FUN, and PERSONAL. It's like talking to your best friend. We use emoji's and exclamation points and are very personal. We take the opposite approach of large corporations that give robotic, frustrating, not very helpful support. You need to be intuitive and have strong emotional intelligence to feel what the students are needing when they communicate.



• More creativity: there is more freedom to adjust your responses for each scenario. This is more challenging than just following strict templates, but also more stimulating and exciting.



• Impact: your actions will have a big impact on the business, which you will clearly see



• Feedback: your feedback on what we are doing well, and what could be better, is not only valued, but is mandatory! We want everyone here thinking as if they were the business owner.





ABOUT YOU

• Perfect written English

• Available full-time

• High emotional intelligence

• Flexible

• Quick learner

• Thrives in fast-paced, quickly changing environment

• Desire to always seek better ways to do things, including finding ways to use A.I. tools

• Happy to be on the phone when needed (which is rarely)

• Tremendous attention to detail - gets things right the first time

• PRO-ACTIVE. Always looking for ways to improve.

• Meticulous, organized, methodical. Almost never misses a detail.

• Critical, creative thinker. Not robotic. Always looking for better ways to do things. Always considering all the angles of a task.

• Not afraid to share your opinion - we NEED to hear your opinion on the approaches that we take so that we can improve.

• Able to ask clarifying questions in order to get tasks right the first time

• Strong communicator

• Makes decisions independently

• Quick learner

• Fast computer

• Fast, dependable Internet

• Bonus if you have experience or interest in salsa or bachata or any dance!





APPLY NOW. FORMAT YOUR APPLICATION AS A NUMBERED LIST WITH THE FOLLOWING:

1. A brief intro about yourself



2. Are you happy to be on the phone when needed?



3. This is a fast-paced environment with quickly changing conditions. You’ll need to be able to think quickly on your feet and track dozens of moving pieces at once with precision and accuracy. This role is NOT for people who feel overly anxious in this type of environment. Apply only if you thrive in such an environment. Based on this information, do you still feel this role is a good fit for you?



4. Complete this form: 
/
. Write in the subject line of your application the answers to the assessment in the link above: 1. Grammar score 2. Typing WPM 3. Numbers assessment answer (regarding achieving $2,000 profit) 4. Highest level of formal education



6. Any customer support experience? If yes, please describe.



7. Which of the two scheduling options do you prefer?



8. These positions are competitive, often with hundreds of applicants. What is your competitive requested monthly payment for this full-time position?





(Don't
 us off the platform or message on social media - your application will be disqualified. Don't worry, we personally check every application here)



Looking forward to meeting you!

title: Sales Representative (Excellent English and Phone Skills)
description:
We are a UK based beauty distributor.



We are seeking a Sales Representative with excellent English and phone skills to join our team. As a Sales Representative, you will be responsible for generating new leads, following up on existing leads, and closing sales over the phone. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in sales, outstanding communication skills, and the ability to build strong relationships with customers.



Responsibilities:



1. Proactively seek new business opportunities through outbound sales calls

2. Respond promptly to inbound sales inquiries and provide exceptional customer service

3. Conduct product demonstrations and presentations over the phone

4. Build and maintain strong customer relationships

6. Maintain accurate and up-to-date customer records in our CRM system

If you are a motivated self-starter with a passion for sales and excellent English and phone skills, we encourage you to apply for this position. title: Organic Growth SEO Specialist - Full Ownership
description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing tutoring company startup providing high-quality, personalised tuition. We are not an EdTech platform — we are a service-based tutoring business, and our growth depends heavily on strong, trustworthy organic visibility on Google.



Role Overview

- This role is for someone who wants to own SEO completely.

- We are looking for a self-starter SEO Specialist who will treat SEO as their own baby. You will be responsible for thinking, planning, executing, testing, and improving everything related to organic search — without needing detailed instructions or constant oversight.

- If you wait to be told what to do, this role is not for you.

- If you enjoy spotting opportunities, creating strategies, and pushing ideas forward independently, you will thrive here.



What Ownership Means in This Role

- You own the entire SEO function end-to-end

- You identify opportunities before being asked

- You propose strategies, experiments, and improvements proactively

- You take responsibility for results, not just tasks

- You think beyond rankings and focus on business impact



Key Responsibilities

- Take full ownership of organic SEO and Google presence

- Create and execute a long-term organic growth strategy for a tutoring business

- Proactively generate and implement SEO ideas, strategies, and experiments

- Perform ongoing keyword research (subjects, tutoring services, locations, parent/student intent)

- Optimise all on-page SEO (content structure, metadata, internal linking)

- Lead content-driven SEO, including suggesting new pages and topics

- Own local SEO strategy (Google Business Profile, location pages, reviews)

- Identify and implement technical SEO improvements independently

- Build natural, high-quality backlinks using ethical strategies

- Monitor performance using Google Search Console and Google Analytics

- Translate data into clear recommendations and actions

- Continuously refine strategy based on results and algorithm changes



Who We’re Looking For

You are someone who:

- Treats SEO like a product you own, not a checklist you follow

- Is naturally proactive, curious, and idea-driven

- Works comfortably without micromanagement

- Spots gaps, opportunities, and weaknesses and acts on them

- Thinks strategically but is also hands-on with execution

- Is confident suggesting improvements — even when they weren’t requested



Required Experience & Skills

- Proven experience owning and growing organic SEO

- Strong understanding of Google’s ranking systems

- Experience with service-based or local businesses

- Confident using SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, GSC, GA4)

- Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset

- Clear communication and ability to justify ideas with data



Nice to Have

- Experience in tutoring or education services

- Local or multi-location SEO experience

- Multilingual SEO experience

- Understanding of UX and conversion optimisation



What We Offer

- High trust and autonomy

- Freedom to build and own the SEO strategy

- Direct impact on company growth

- Flexible working setup



Long-term opportunity as the business scales



How to Apply

Please share:

- Examples where you fully owned SEO

- Results you achieved through your own strategy and initiatives

- How you typically d
 what to work on without being told

Twilio / GHL phone systems

webhooks and integrations


 or Zapier

What Success Looks Like

GHL becomes the single source of truth for all client data

title: AI Web App Developer — Full Stack (React + Node + Claude API)
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



We are building an AI-powered web app for a well-established online business in the creator space. The business has been operating for 5 years with a large, engaged audience and existing revenue — this is a funded project, not a startup idea.



We need a skilled developer who has genuine experience building AI-powered applications — specifically using large language model APIs (we are using Anthropic Claude). This is not a generic web app build. The core product is AI-driven and the quality of the AI integration is what makes or breaks it.



This is a long-term opportunity. If you are the right person, this role grows with the product.



---



WHAT YOU WILL BUILD



A mobile-first web application with the following core components:



- AI content generation engine using the Anthropic Claude API

- Per-user profile system that stores voice, tone, and preference data to personalise AI outputs over time

- Daily interactive prompt flow — guided questions that feed into AI-generated content

- Image upload and storage

- Social media publishing integration (Buffer or Later API —
 and Instagram)

- User authentication and subscription billing (Stripe)

- Simple database architecture for storing user data, content history, and ideas

-
 platform integration (MailerLite API)



The frontend is React or Next.js. The backend is Node.js or Python — we are open to recommendation. Hosting on Cloudflare or Vercel.



---



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



Essential:

- Proven experience building with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar)

- Strong full-stack skills — React frontend, Node.js or Python backend

- Experience with third-party API integrations

- Ability to think about product architecture, not just execute a spec

- Good written English — you will need to communicate clearly with a non-technical founder

- Attention to detail and ability to work independently



Nice to have:

- Experience with Anthropic Claude API specifically

- Experience with Buffer or Later API

- Experience with Stripe subscription billing

- Understanding of prompt engineering and context management



---



WHAT WE ARE NOT LOOKING FOR



We are not looking for someone who will apply without reading this posting carefully. We are not looking for the cheapest option. We are looking for the right person who takes pride in their work and wants to be part of building something genuinely useful from the ground up.



If you are copy-pasting a generic application, please do not apply.



---



HOW TO APPLY



We do not accept applications through OnlineJobs.ph directly.



To be considered, you must complete our application form here:







The form takes approximately 10 minutes. It asks about your relevant experience, your approach to AI app development, and includes a short technical question. Applications submitted outside of this form will not be reviewed.



One last thing... when you fill in the application form, there is a question that asks for a specific word. The word is easel. 



This tells us you read the full posting. Applications without this word in the correct field will be automatically disqualified.



COMPENSATION



Competitive rate based on experience. This is a long-term engagement with room to grow. Please include your expected monthly rate based on part time hours in the application form.



-



We look forward to hearing from serious applicants only.

title: Sales Appointment Setter
description:
As a Cold Caller, our primary role will be to generate new business leads through cold calling and outreach efforts. You’ll be instrumental in expanding our customer base by identifying and connecting with potential clients in need of our digital marketing services.





## Key Responsibilities

- **Outbound Calling:** Initiate calls to prospective clients using our contact lists, introducing them to services and value proposition.

- **Lead Qualification:** Engage with decision-makers, qualify leads, and assess their needs to determine if they align with the company's offerings.

- **Appointment Scheduling:** Schedule follow-up calls or consultations between prospects and the sales team to close deals.

- **Maintain CRM Records:** Accurately log and update lead and prospect information in our CRM system.

- **Achieve Targets:** Meet or exceed monthly lead generation and call volume targets.

- **Feedback Reporting:** Provide feedback to the marketing and sales team about prospects' reactions, objections, and potential areas of improvement.



## Requirements

- **Experience:** Prior experience in cold calling or telesales is preferred, but not required. Experience in a digital marketing environment is a plus.

- **Communication Skills:** Excellent verbal communication and persuasion skills. Ability to establish rapport quickly with potential clients.

- **Resilience:** Ability to handle rejection and stay motivated in a goal-driven environment.

- **Tech-Savvy:** Comfortable using CRM software and other tools for lead tracking and outreach.

- **Time Management:** Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to handle multiple tasks efficiently.

- **Self-Starter:** Proactive and driven to meet and exceed goals independently.



Kindly send your updated CV along with a 1-2min self intro. Thank you!

title: Community Outreach Appointment Setter (Voice Role)
description:

What Makes This Role Different:

This is not a scripted cold-calling job.



We are looking for someone who can have natural conversations, connect with people quickly, and speak with confidence and authenticity.



You should feel comfortable speaking with business owners, building rapport, and respectfully asking if they would be open to learning about a community initiative designed to support local businesses.



Responsibilities:

- Reach out to local businesses by phone

- Introduce a community-focused initiative

- Ask business owners if they would be open to a short conversation

- Schedule brief appointments when appropriate

- Help build positive relationships with businesses in targeted neighborhoods



What We’re Looking For

- Excellent spoken English

- Clear and confident speaking voice

- Neutral or American-style accent

- Friendly, professional, and respectful tone

- Strong interpersonal communication skills

- Ability to build rapport quickly

- Positive and persistent mindset

- Comfortable having natural conversations without reading from a script

This role is ideal for someone who genuinely enjoys talking with people, building relationships, and sounding confident and natural in conversation. Because you will be speaking with U.S. business owners, strong spoken English and a clear, easy-to-understand conversational style are very important for this role.



Schedule:

Night shift (Philippines time) covering U.S. Pacific Standard Time (PST) business hours.



How to Apply:

Please submit the following:

- A short introduction about yourself.

- A 60 second voice recording answering this question:

"Imagine you are calling a local business owner to introduce a community project and asking if they would have a few minutes to learn more."

- A short written answer to this question: Why do you think local businesses value being connected to their community?



Attention to Detail:

Attention to detail matters in this role.

When submitting your application, please begin your message with the word “Community” so we know you read the full job post.



Opportunity for Growth:

We are building something long-term that connects and supports local business communities.

The outreach begins with Tierrasanta, but we will quickly expand into other targeted neighborhoods such as Kearny Mesa and beyond.



As the project grows, there will be additional opportunities for those who demonstrate strong communication skills, professionalism, and commitment to helping local business communities succeed.

title: **CLAUDE CODE DEVELOPER — AI Content Production Pipeline (LinkedIn Agency)**
description:
**Overview**



We're a LinkedIn content and lead generation agency working with 47 clients. Our entire operation runs on Claude AI — we've built a suite of 40+ custom skills (structured markdown prompt files) that handle everything from content strategy to visual production briefs to carousel generation.



The problem: our production workflow involves too many manual steps. A tea
mber runs each skill sequentially, copies outputs between tools, creates folders manually, and pushes briefs into Canva by hand. At 47 clients producing multiple content assets per month each, that's a lot of admin that shouldn't require a human.



We want to build a Claude Code pipeline that chains these steps automatically — so our designer focuses on QA and finishing, not copy-pasting and folder management.



---



**Current manual workflow**



Here's what happens right now, step by step:



1. Approved written content is taken from the client project

2. Tea
mber opens Claude, loads the Visual Architect skill, inputs the content ? receives structured Nano Banana image prompts (formatted for Manus image generation)

3. Tea
mber opens Claude, loads the Carousel Visualiser skill, inputs the content ? receives a carousel brief with slide-by-slide copy and layout direction

4. Carousel brief is manually pushed into Canva via the Canva MCP connector

5. Nano Banana prompts are manually taken into Manus for image generation

6. Finished assets are manually dropped into the correct Google Drive client folder



We want steps 2, 3, 4, and 6 automated. Step 5 (Manus image generation) remains manual for now.



---



**What we want to build**



A Claude Code script (or lightweight CLI tool) with a simple trigger. The designer inputs:



- Client name

- Approved written content (pasted or file path)



And the pipeline automatically:



1. **Runs the Visual Architect skill** against the content ? produces formatted Nano Banana prompts ready for Manus, saved to a structured output file

2. **Runs the Carousel Visualiser skill** against the content ? produces carousel brief with slide copy, layout direction, and content QA output

3. **Pushes the carousel brief into Canva** via Canva MCP — creating the design file in the correct client workspace

4. **Locates or creates the correct Google Drive client folder** and drops all outputs (prompts file, carousel brief, Canva link) into it, correctly named



The designer then opens the folder, takes the Nano Banana prompts into Manus manually, and does final QA on the Canva output.



---



**The tech stack**



- **Claude Code** — primary development environment

- **Anthropic API** — claude-sonnet
, skills loaded as system prompts

- **Canva MCP** — already connected (
/mcp), used for pushing carousel briefs and creating design files

- **Google Drive MCP** — already connected (
 — Drive access enabled), used for folder management and file storage

- **Our skill files** — 40+ structured .md files, two of which are central to this pipeline (Visual Architect v4.0, Carousel Visualiser v2.1). You'll receive full access

- **Google Drive** — client folders already exist and follow a consistent naming convention



---



**Specific deliverables**



1. A working Claude Code pipeline script that executes the full automated sequence described above

2. A simple interface for the designer — ideally a CLI prompt or basic input form. No complex UI required

3. Error handling — if a client folder doesn't exist in Drive, create it. If Canva push fails, log it and flag to the designer

4. Output logging — a simple run log per execution (client name, date, assets created, Canva link, Drive folder path)

5. Documentation — a plain-English setup and usage guide written for a non-technical designer. Step by step, no assumptions

6. A short walkthrough video of the working pipeline (Loom is fine)



---



**What we're not building**



- A full web app or dashboard

- Anything that touches Manus (that stays manual for now)

- Anything client-facing

- Complex UI — this is an internal production tool



---



**Scale context**



47 clients. Each client produces approximately:

- 10 personal LinkedIn posts per month

- 4 carousels per month

- 8 company page posts per month



The pipeline needs to handle single-client runs and ideally batch runs (multiple clients queued). Not all of this in v1 — but worth knowing so you don't architect something that can't scale.



---



**Who we're looking for**



- Solid experience with Claude Code and the Anthropic API

- Has worked with MCP integrations before — ideally Canva or Google Drive

- Comfortable reading and working with structured markdown prompt files (our skills)

- Builds things that non-technical people can actually use

- Asks good questions before building, not after

- Available for a short discovery call before starting



---



**To apply**



Please answer these three questions in your proposal:



1. Have you built anything with Claude Code before? Describe the project and what it did.

2. Have you worked with MCP integrations — specifically Canva MCP or Google Drive MCP? What was the use case?

3. How would you approach chaining multiple large system prompts (each 1,000–3,000 words) through the Anthropic API without hitting context issues?



Proposals without answers to all three will not be considered.

Let me know how much  you think the project would cost

title: Performance Creative Strategist
description:
About Us



[vrsn] is a Hong Kong-based footwear brand redefining what pain-relief shoes can be: functional, stylish, and built for everyday life. After a successful Kickstarter launch (
) in 2024, we’re building out our global eCommerce business at [vrsn.co] (
). 



The Role



We’re looking for a Performance Creative Strategist to develop and oversee ad concepts from idea to launch. Your goal is simple: create ads that grab attention and drive revenue. This role blends creative ideation, structured testing, and close collaboration with editors, designers, and media buyers to turn concepts into high-performing campaigns.



What You’ll Do



- Research trends, competitors, and performance data to generate ad concepts, and maintain a balanced pipeline of both best-practice and outside-the-box ideas

- Write scripts, outlines, and briefs for UGC creators, editors, and designers, and collaborate during production to ensure concepts are executed effectively

- Partner with the media buyer to hand off creative, review results, and join feedback loops to identify what’s working and refine future concepts



What We’re Looking For



- 2–5 years experience in performance creative strategy for paid social (Meta/TikTok), either agency or in-house, ideally with DTC/eCommerce brands

- Working knowledge of ad operations, testing cadence, and how creative scales on Meta — you understand creative testing frameworks, fatigue curves, and what it takes to move metrics like CTR, hook rates and ultimately CPA/ROAS

- Strong mix of creativity and analytical rigor: able to propose fresh concepts and back them with data-driven rationale

- Experience scripting UGC content, briefing creators/editors, and collaborating hands-on with creative teams

- Passion for testing, learning, and iterating quickly; excellent communicator and collaborator



**Nice to Have**



- Active user of AI tools and workflows (ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, Runway, etc.) to accelerate ideation, scripting, research, and production — you look for ways to use AI to do better work, faster

- Experience across multiple ad platforms (TikTok, YouTube, etc.)



To Apply



Please share:



- A portfolio or case studies showing ad concepts you’ve developed and the results they achieved

- A short note on how you approach balancing data-driven rationale with creative “outside the box” thinking

- (Optional) One example of how you've used AI tools to improve your creative workflow

title: Content Operations Specialist (AI-Assisted Text Processing)
description:
Content Operations Specialist (AI-Assisted Text Processing)



Location: Remote (Work from Home)

Job Type: Full time

Experience: 1–3 years (or equivalent experience with structured content and AI tools)



About Us:



We are building a new kind of knowledge platform focused on how people understand, organize, and engage with writing. Our work goes beyond traditional content systems, aiming to create more structured, meaningful, and connected ways to work with information.



Our goal is to build the underlying structure that allows knowledge to be captured, organized, and connected in more intelligent and flexible ways.



Role Description:



We are looking for a highly detail-oriented Content Operations Specialist to help transform raw text from external sources into clean, structured content for our platform. This role relies heavily on using AI tools to process, refine, and restructure documents according to precise guidelines.



You will be responsible for guiding AI tools, validating their output, correcting subtle errors, and ensuring that every document meets strict formatting and structural standards. This is not a software development role. It is an operations role focused on accuracy, consistency, and high-quality execution.



Key Responsibilities:



Use AI tools to clean, format, and restructure text documents from external sources.



Remove unwanted characters, artifacts, and formatting inconsistencies.



Apply detailed formatting and structural rules to documents.



Convert content into standardized JSON formats used by the platform.



Review AI-generated output for subtle errors and correct them manually when needed.



Ensure consistency and accuracy across large batches of documents.



Follow and refine written content-processing procedures.



Flag edge cases and ambiguities in source material or formatting rules.



Qualifications:



Strong ability to follow detailed, nuanced instructions.



Comfort working with AI tools as part of daily workflows.



Exceptional attention to detail and consistency.



Strong written communication skills.



Ability to stay focused on repetitive, precision-based tasks.



Comfort working with structured formats like JSON (no coding required, but accuracy is essential).



Organized, reliable, and process-oriented.



Interest in knowledge platforms, structured content, or information systems is a plus.



How to Apply:



If you enjoy working with AI tools, care deeply about precision, and like turning messy information into clean, structured data, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your resume along with a short explanation of any experience you have working with structured content, AI tools, or detail-focused operational work.

title: AI GENERALIST - HOSPITALITY INDUSTRY
description:
Are you someone who does not just use AI -- but thinks in AI?

 

Residentas is an international hospitality company with premium properties in Lisbon and the USA. We are building one of the most AI-integrated operations in the short-term rental space, and we need someone who lives at that frontier with us.

This is not a traditional role. We are not looking for a VA or a marketer or an analyst. We are looking for an AI Generalist -- someone who sees a problem and immediately asks: how do I use AI to solve this better, faster, and at a higher standard than a human team could alone.

 

If you think AI is a tool you use occasionally, this role is not for you. If AI is how you think -- read on.

 

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING:

+ Identifying repetitive or manual processes across our operations and automating them using AI tools and workflows. +Building internal tools, templates and systems using Claude, ChatGPT, Replit or similar platforms. 

+ Supporting our guest experience, listings,
 funnels and market research with AI-generated output that actually meets a luxury brand standard. 

+ Translating business problems into AI prompts, workflows and solutions -- without waiting to be told how. 

+Documenting what you build so the team can use it without you holding their hand. 

 

QUALITIES WE VALUE:

 

--Systems Thinking: You do not just complete tasks. You redesign them.

 

--AI Fluency: Claude, ChatGPT, Replit, Zapier, Make -- you move between tools and you know when to use which.

 

--Judgment: You know when AI output is good enough and when it is not. You do not ship garbage.

 

--Ownership: You treat problems like they are yours to solve.

 

--Integrity: You say what you think, especially when something is not working.

 

REQUIREMENTS:

 

-- You have used AI tools to build or automate something real -- not just write
s. 

-- Strong written English. Your output will represent a premium brand. 

-- Comfortable learning new platforms fast. We move quickly. 

-- Self-directed. You do not need daily instructions to make progress.

 -- Bonus: experience in hospitality, property management or e-commerce operations.

 

WHAT WILL DISQUALIFY YOU IMMEDIATELY:

 

+Applying without examples of real AI-assisted work you have produced.

+Generic applications that do not follow the instructions below.

+Treating AI as a shortcut rather than a standard-raiser.

 

THE BENEFITS:

 

+Starting pay: 30,000 PHP per month.

 

+100% Remote work.

 

+Direct line to the founder from day one.

 

+You will be building systems that define how this company operates for years.

 

+You will work with tools and methods most companies will not adopt for years.

 

HOW TO APPLY:

Send me a direct message here on OnlineJobs. In the subject line, include the code AG1. I will then send you a link to our official application form. On that form, in the extra comments field, write AG1.

title: Photo Editing QA and Customer Support
description:
ABOUT REAL TOURS



Real Tours is a fast-growing real estate media company delivering professional photography editing, virtual staging, 3D tours, and listing media to real estate agents and brokers. We pride ourselves on fast turnarounds, exceptional quality, and client relationships that keep agents coming back listing after listing.



ROLE OVERVIEW



We are looking for a versatile QA & Client Support Specialist who can operate across both quality assurance and client-facing support functions. In this hybrid role, you will review edited media for quality before delivery while also serving as a point of contact for client inquiries, re-edit requests, and order updates. This position is ideal for someone who is both a sharp visual reviewer and a confident communicator — someone who understands what great real estate media looks like and can represent that standard to clients.



QUALITY ASSURANCE RESPONSIBILITIES



Review edited real estate photo batches for exposure consistency, color accuracy, sky replacement quality, horizon alignment, and overall presentation

Do virtual staging deliverables for realistic staging, proper scale, material texture, and lighting match to the actual room

Do item removal edits for seamless surface reconstruction with no visible patching or artifacts

Inspecta and create Matterport, Zillow 3D Home, and CubiCasa floor plan and 3D tour

Revise rejected work for clients and their needs

Track and document recurring quality issues to support editor coaching and process improvement



CUSTOMER SUPPORT RESPONSIBILITIES



Serve as a primary point of contact for real estate agent clients via
, Slack, and internal platforms

Communicate order statuses, delivery timelines, and proactively notify clients of any delays

Process re-edit requests and relay precise client feedback to the editing team

Manage client expectations around same-day and next-day delivery SLAs

Escalate unresolved issues or pattern complaints to the appropriate team lead

Support new client onboarding and guide agents through the revision and delivery process



REQUIREMENTS



1+ years of hands-on experience in real estate photo editing, virtual staging, or media post-production — you must be able to identify AND fix common editing issues, not just flag them

Proficient in Adobe Photoshop — able to perform exposure corrections, sky replacements, color grading adjustments, and clean item removals independently when needed

Advanced virtual staging knowledge: understanding of furniture scale, perspective matching, lighting consistency, shadow realism, and material textures

Advanced item removal skills: ability to reconstruct floors, walls, ceilings, and surfaces convincingly with no visible patching, cloning artifacts, or texture mismatches

Familiarity with real estate photo editing standards: sky replacements, virtual staging, item removals, twilight edits, and exposure corrections

Strong written English communication skills — able to write clearly to both clients and editors

Highly self-managed and organized with the ability to juggle QA reviews and open client tickets simultaneously

Experience with Slack, 
, Notion, or similar remote work tools

Comfortable with fast-paced, high-volume workloads and same-day turnaround expectations



NICE TO HAVE



Direct experience with Aryeo, Matterport, CubiCasa, or Zillow 3D Home

Prior work with a real estate photography/media company

Experience with AI editing tools and awareness of AI artifact detection

Background in virtual staging review or advanced retouching QA



TOOLS YOU WILL USE



Adobe Photoshop / Lightroom

Aryeo

Matterport

Zillow 3D Home

CubiCasa

AI Editing Tools

Slack


 / Notion





HOW TO APPLY

To apply, please submit your resume and a brief cover note describing your relevant experience.

Include links to any relevant work samples, portfolios, or past projects where applicable.

title: Full-Time Video Editor + Social Media Poster for Australian Fitness Brand        (Long-Term, $X USD/Week)
description:
Hi, I'm Sam — founder of Built Body Transformations. We coach

men in their 30s, 40s, and 50s who've built great careers but

let their bodies fall behind. We've coached over 1,700 men.



I need ONE full-time editor + poster to own my entire content

pipeline — short-form Reels and TikToks, long-form YouTube,

and paid ad creatives. My face is on every video.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN



- Edit 8-15 short-form vertical videos per week (Reels, TikTok,

  YouTube Shorts) from my raw footage and podcast clips

- Edit 3 long-form YouTube videos per week (8-15 min)

- Edit paid ad creatives for our coaching offer (3-6 per week)

- Edit VSLs for our coaching offer

- Schedule and publish all videos across IG, TikTok, YouTube,

  LinkedIn using a scheduler (Metricool/Later/Buffer)

- Light thumbnail design (I will approve)

- Weekly report on view counts and what's working



REQUIREMENTS



- 2+ years editing short-form for personal brands or creators

- Portfolio with proof — at least 3 short-form vertical edits

  with visible view counts (50K+ ideal)

- Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve fluent

- After Effects basics

- Native-level written and spoken English

- Available 4+ hours overlap with Sydney time (GMT+10)

- Reliable 50+ Mbps internet and backup power



NICE TO HAVE



- Fitness or wellness content experience

- Experience editing paid Meta/YouTube ads (NOT just organic)

- Scheduling tool experience (Metricool, Later, Buffer)



DO NOT APPLY IF



- This is a side hustle for you

- You can't handle revisions and tight deadlines

- You haven't actually edited short-form for a creator before

- You only know CapCut (we need real Premiere or Resolve work)



YOU SHOULD APPLY IF



- You're obsessed with the craft of short-form

- You take feedback well and iterate fast

- You want a long-term, stable, full-time role

- You actually train / care about fitness (bonus)



PAY



$X USD/week, paid weekly via Wise. Long-term role, paid time off,

13th-month bonus, performance bonuses for videos that cross

100K views.



PROCESS



1. Send your portfolio link (NOT a resume, NOT a PDF)

2. Answer these 3 questions in your message:

   - What's the best Reel you've edited and why did it work?

   - What software do you prefer and why?

   - If I gave you a 2-min raw talking-head clip, describe

     your first 3 edit moves.

3. Subject line MUST start with: "BUILT EDITOR — [your name]"

4. Tell me your favourite food at the end of your message.

   (This is a filter. Applications without it will be deleted.)



Top candidates will be invited to a paid trial edit ($50 USD).

Top 2-3 trial winners will have a 30-min Zoom interview with me.

Final pick gets a 2-week paid trial at full rate, then full hire.

title: Senior Digital Marketing Strategist & Client Success Lead
description:
***Must be available Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST)



About the Role
